title: 清洁女工之死
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\209-清洁女工之死-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Hercule Poirot and Inspector Spence discuss several women from past criminal cases to find potential connections to the death of Mrs. McGinty. Spence provides details on Eva Kane, who fled the country after Alfred Craig's conviction and changed her name to Evelyn Hope; while the public viewed her as an innocent victim, Detective Inspector Trelawney suspected she was an actress who helped Craig dispose of his wife. They also discuss Janice Courtland, who allegedly incited her husband to violence to marry a rich man, and Lily Garfield, a girl sent to an orphanage after killing her aunt with a meat cleaver—the same type of weapon allegedly used to kill Mrs. McKinty.

The two men then analyze the residents of Broadhurst to see if any match these profiles. They consider the Wetherby family, where Mrs. McGinty worked. Mrs. Wetherby is of a similar age to Eva Kane, and her daughter is of a similar age to Eva's daughter; Poirot notes the daughter's hatred for her stepfather suggests motives involving family secrets. They also consider Mrs. Broadhurst, who matches Eva Kane's age but is unlikely to be the killer due to her severe arthritis and domineering personality.

Finally, they examine three women in their thirties who could be Lily Garfield or Eva Kane's daughter: Dierdre Henderson, Dr. Rendell's wife, and Eve Carpenter. Dr. Rendell's wife is blonde, matching Lily Garfield's description. Eve Carpenter is also blonde with captivating blue eyes, though Spence notes her disoriented, moth-like behavior when she recently fled a room.
